title: The Coordinator's Memos
description: Two internal memos pinned to the community center bulletin board. The Masterites are off-profile. Quality of attention cannot be measured. The Arbiter flagged the same gap.

steps.text: The community center bulletin board was in the hallway outside the administrative wing. It was the kind of board that existed in every building of this type: laminated class schedules, a signup sheet for a potluck that had already occurred, a flyer for a fundraiser that still needed three volunteers.
Between the potluck signup and the volunteer flyer, pinned with a single pushpin: two printed memos. The header read: INTERNAL STATUS COMMUNICATION. COORDINATOR'S OFFICE. NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: They pinned them to the community bulletin board.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Someone did. Not necessarily the Coordinator.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Read them.

steps.text: The Mage unpinned the first memo.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: INTERNAL STATUS COMMUNICATION — Coordinator's Office. SUBJECT: Assessment Update, Active Subjects.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: Warden (World 1): Demonstrating unusual self-reflection. Engagement pattern deviates from established baseline. The cave confrontation was not on-profile. I have flagged this for review. It may be within acceptable variance. I am less certain of this than I expected to be.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: Arbiter: Passed on a curve I calibrated to be uncurvable. I am revising the assessment rubric. This is the third revision. I am beginning to suspect the rubric.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: Tempo: Subject was observed during cooldown phase thinking about beginnings. This is off-profile. Tempos think about speed. This one is thinking about origins. I do not know what to do with this data.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: These subjects are ahead of schedule. I am noting this without alarm, because alarm is not a useful operational state. I am noting it anyway.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: One subject is maintaining detailed notes. Her pattern recognition is exceptional. She will likely reach conclusions I had hoped to defer to a later phase.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: I do not find this alarming. I find it interesting. This is also unusual. End of memo.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: She's watching the Masterites. Not just us.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: 'She will likely reach conclusions I had hoped to defer.' Which one of us is taking notes?

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: The Warden was off-profile. The Arbiter. The Tempo. The Masterites are changing.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: She's noting it the way you note a deviation in a machine. Not distressed. Observing.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: She finds it interesting that she's finding it interesting.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: That's recursive.

steps.text: The Mage unpinned the second memo.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: INTERNAL STATUS COMMUNICATION — Coordinator's Office. SUBJECT: Incident Report — Oak Street Garage Sale. Sweater Acquisition.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: The enchanted sweater was programmed with a deterrence function: active resistance, designed to discourage acquisition and observe subject response to a minor obstacle with non-zero effort cost.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: The Cleric acquired it anyway. She kept it. She is wearing it. The deterrence function did not deter. This was not a designed outcome.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: The sweater is not meaningful. It is a garment with an embroidered bear. And yet.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: I have been attempting to locate the variable that accounts for her decision. The deterrence was clear. The effort cost was non-trivial. The item is of low strategic value. Her persistence does not map to any optimization model I am currently running.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: The provisional term I am using is 'quality of attention.' She attends to things. Not strategically. Not instrumentally. She simply notices, and the noticing appears to carry weight for her independent of outcome.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: My models are built on outcomes and optimization. They do not have a column for 'attended to it anyway.' I do not know what to do with 'quality of attention' as a metric.

lines.speaker: MEMO
lines.text: The Arbiter has flagged the same gap in his assessment rubric, from a different angle. We are converging on the same problem. End of memo.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: She wrote a memo about my sweater.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: She wrote a memo about why she cannot explain why you kept it.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: There is a difference.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: I kept it because I liked the embroidered bear.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: That's what she can't model. 'I liked the bear.' There's no optimization function for that. No rubric. It's just— you noticed it, and it mattered to you.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Two Masterites. Different worlds, different roles. Both hit the same wall.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The Arbiter's rubric. The Coordinator's models. Different instruments, same gap.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: Which means whatever they're trying to measure, they still can't measure it. After all of this. After everything calibrated to produce data.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: She finds it interesting that she can't account for us.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I find that interesting.

steps.text: The Mage folded both memos and put them in his jacket pocket. The potluck signup sheet still needed three more volunteers. Nobody added a name.
